Summary: A new California bill, Assembly Bill 2071, aims to teach students digital wellness in health classes, focusing on responsible technology use rather than outright bans. This initiative is driven by student concerns about mental health impacts from social media and AI, and follows legal actions against tech companies. The bill, supported by students and legislators, would require the California Department of Education to develop a plan by 2028 for teaching topics like healthy screen habits and online safety. It also emphasizes the need for equitable access to this education, particularly for students from low-income communities.

The 17th annual Noyo Food Forest Earth Day Festival in Fort Bragg, CA, focused on environmental education and sustainability. The event featured over 50 organizations and vendors, including C&S Waste Redwood Waste Solutions and Mendocino Spartan 4-H, offering activities and information on recycling, regenerative agriculture, and local food systems. Noyo Food Forest, which provides hands-on training and internships for high school students, supports farm-to-school programs and educational gardens. The festival also included performances by the Mendocino Center for Circus Arts and highlighted the importance of community involvement and volunteers.

The Mendocino Voice is a nonprofit news site focused on local stories for Mendocino County, California. A recent article reports that Fort Bragg police are seeking public assistance in locating a missing 78-year-old man, Philip Cardman. The website also encourages readers to support local journalism through donations and newsletter sign-ups, highlighting its commitment to informing the community. The content mentions police, readers, and the general public as social groups.

The Flock Together Festival, celebrating sheep and agrarian culture, will be held at Ridgewood Ranch in Willits on Saturday, April 25th. The event offers a variety of activities for all ages, including sheep shearing, sheepdog demonstrations, fiber arts, and a ranch-to-table lunch. Visitors can experience rural life firsthand and learn about Mendocino County's sheep ranching history. The festival aims to foster connection, curiosity, and joy by bringing together farmers, ranchers, and the community.

The Mendocino Voice reports on the apprehension of a 16-year-old male juvenile suspected in the shooting death of a 38-year-old Covelo woman. The suspect is also linked to a domestic violence incident involving a 15-year-old female victim, who is a relative of the deceased. The article highlights the importance of local journalism and encourages community support for The Mendocino Voice. It also mentions law enforcement agencies, including the Mendocino County Sheriff's Office and Oroville Police Department, and provides contact information for tips.

The webpage summarizes a candidate forum for Mendocino County District 3 Supervisor. Key topics discussed include the Potter Valley Project, Jackson Demonstration State Forest, and wildfire preparedness. Candidates Buffey Wright Bourassa, Russell Green, Eric Hart, and Clay Romero participated. The article also mentions a civil lawsuit against Russell Green and the involvement of various community organizations in organizing the event.

The Mendocino Voice reports on the arrest of Raymond Bowes, a Covelo man, on suspicion of burglary and robbery. The incident involved an alleged assault and theft from a male victim, and a woman who was initially reported as kidnapped was found unharmed. Bowes, who was out on bail for a previous felony, faces additional charges. The article also highlights the importance of local news and encourages reader support for The Mendocino Voice.

The Mendocino Voice is a local, nonprofit news source for Mendocino County, California, focusing on community stories. Recent articles cover local events like festivals and fundraisers, crime reports, political forums, and environmental issues. The site also features local businesses, arts and culture, and animal adoption. They encourage reader support and offer a newsletter for updates.
